summary: Full E2E validation of MemoryVault (backend REST + MCP JSON-RPC + React dashboard). 25/25 pytest cases pass and 12/12 frontend flows pass. Auth (register/login/me/admin), ingestion + Claude Opus 4.5 fact extraction, high-stakes gating (health/contact -> pending), supersession, pending confirm/reject, semantic search + scoring, token-budgeted context pack, decay, JSON export, vault isolation between users, MCP initialize/tools_list/tools_call with Bearer mcp_token, unauthorized 401, and MCP token regeneration all verified against the live public URL.

minor.issue: tokens_used can slightly exceed token_budget by a few tokens because the header 'User Memory Context' is always counted but the section headers ('## Profile', '## Relevant Memory') and blank separators are NOT counted toward `used` during greedy fill (see memory.build_context_pack). In practice output stays within budget for normal budgets (>=300), and the returned tokens_used field is computed on the final assembled string so is honest. Not blocking, but the fill loop underestimates cost.

issues: Radix a11y console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Add a <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby to the Sheet/Dialog content in FactDetail.jsx.

The two-line headline 'A memory your AI
actually keeps.' is read as 'A memory your AIactually keeps.' when the
is stripped by screen readers or when parsed to text; visual is fine (line break), but the tags render without whitespace and screen readers may lose the break. Consider 'A memory your AI\n' or hidden space.
Overview stats show em-dash '—' while data is loading. Consider a skeleton placeholder or 0 to avoid confusing 'no data' impression.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to FactDetail's Sheet/Dialog to silence the Radix a11y warning.
OPTIONAL: In memory.build_context_pack, include section header token cost in the greedy fill accounting for tighter budget adherence (functional impact minimal).
No blocking issues. App is ready for handoff.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py imports 'uuid' and '_public_user' but does not use them (dead imports). Minor cleanup.

auth.py 'get_current_user' looks up user WITHOUT projecting out 'password_hash' — the password hash is loaded into memory on every authenticated request. Consider projecting {'_id': 0, 'password_hash': 0} for safety in logs/debug.

memory.build_context_pack does not count the '\n\n' separator and section headers ('## Profile', '## Relevant Memory') toward `used`, so it can undercount. In practice budget still holds because approx_tokens returned at the end is honest, but internally the loop could add one extra line beyond target on tight budgets.
server.py CORS uses allow_credentials=False but allow_origins=['*'] which is fine; if you ever want cookies you'll need explicit origins. Currently JWT-in-header only so acceptable.

extractor.py catches broad Exception and returns [] silently on both primary+fallback failure; recommend surfacing model='error' to the client (already returned) and logging with more detail (currently only .error and .warning at logger — good).

auth.py duplicate registration returns 400 with detail; register/login raise HTTPException 400/401 — good use of status codes. Passwords are bcrypt-hashed via bcrypt.gensalt() -> $2b$ prefix (verified working).
mcp.py tools/list returns exactly 6 tools with proper JSON schema. tools/call returns text content with json.dumps(default=str) — good handling for non-serializable types.
No use of MongoDB _id in responses — all queries project {'_id': 0}. Good.
test_credentials: Seeded admin admin@memoryvault.dev / admin123 works. Test users created ad-hoc with TEST_/e2e_ email prefixes.
seed_data_creation: Ad-hoc TEST_* users created by pytest fixtures (per session). Frontend E2E created one e2e_* user and ingested 2 messages under it.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_memoryvault.py (25 tests, parallel via pytest-xdist configured in pytest.ini). LLM extraction via Claude Opus 4.5 works reliably (~2-4s per call). MCP JSON-RPC at POST /api/mcp; tools/call REQUIRES Authorization: Bearer <mcp_token from /api/mcp/info>. High-stakes types are {health, finance, contact} — always gated to pending. Supersession works on same (type, key) pair.
rca of the issue: No production issues. Only minor cosmetic/code-quality suggestions listed under critical_code_review_comments and design_issues.
